ddls:
CREATE TABLE "geospatial_polygons" (
  "polygon_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each polygon */,
  "geometry" TEXT /* Geometric representation of the polygon in spatial format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or metadata about the polygon */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the polygon was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the polygon was last updated */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Category ID for the polygon */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the polygon data (e.g., satellite imagery, GPS tracking) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("polygon_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_geospatial_polygons_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES polygon_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "polygon_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Forest, Urban, Agricultural) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "polygon_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "polygon_id" INTEGER /* ID of the polygon being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the polygon */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the polygon was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access (e.g., user's IP address, accessed features) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polygon_access_logs_polygon_id FOREIGN KEY ("polygon_id") REFERENCES geospatial_polygons ("polygon_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polygon_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its permissions */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was assigned the role */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "polygon_datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "polygon_id" INTEGER /* ID of the polygon in the dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the dataset was uploaded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the dataset was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polygon_datasets_polygon_id FOREIGN KEY ("polygon_id") REFERENCES geospatial_polygons ("polygon_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES polygon_datasets ("dataset_id")
)
